title: Allez Vous

jokes.text: She can be an angry drunk, but we were always close.
jokes.setup: Moira fondly describing her 'dear friend' who is reaching out
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting the friend is an angry drunk while emphasizing their closeness
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Moira's ability to romanticize toxic relationships while admitting uncomfortable truths. Great economy and delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh, it's my dear friend reaching out. We're so close, you know. They mean everything to me.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Though I should mention, they're an angry drunk. But that doesn't change how much I care about them.

jokes.text: That frigid whore!
jokes.setup: Moira learning the package is a cosmetics starter kit
jokes.punchline: Immediate escalation from grateful to viciously insulting her 'dear friend'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Moira's quick turn from gratitude to rage. Perfect character integration and highly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh, how thoughtful! A cosmetics starter kit from my dear friend.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: A *starter* kit? What exactly are you suggesting? That my beauty regimen is somehow... insufficient? That I need to *start* over? How dare you insinuate that my complexion requires remedial intervention, you absolute charlatan. Take your pedestrian drugstore drivel and your thinly veiled insults elsewhere!

jokes.text: Well, apparently you can win an Audi. / What colour?
jokes.setup: David pointing out they could win a car from the pyramid scheme Moira just denounced
jokes.punchline: Moira immediately interested in the car color despite calling it a scam
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work showing Moira's hypocrisy and materialism. The immediate pivot from principles to vanity is perfectly executed.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: But you could win a car.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: What color is it?

jokes.text: don't believe everything you read, dear, please.
jokes.setup: David reads that Kristi Stubbs won a car from the scheme
jokes.punchline: Moira dismisses it while simultaneously getting interested in the scheme herself
jokes.explanation: Good character hypocrisy moment, though less punchy than surrounding jokes. Shows Moira's selective skepticism.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh, that's ridiculous. People don't just win cars from schemes like that.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Although, what exactly is the scheme? How much is the initial investment?

jokes.text: Where does a 92-year-old woman need to go?!
jokes.setup: Johnny complaining about Ray not lending his car because he has to drive his elderly mother
jokes.punchline: Johnny's complete lack of empathy for elderly care needs
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Johnny's self-centeredness, though the setup is somewhat predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Ray won't lend me his car because he has to drive his elderly mother.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I mean, what am I supposed to do? Take the bus like some sort of... person with limited resources?

jokes.text: I don't know, John, perhaps the hospital?
jokes.setup: Johnny's oblivious question about where a 92-year-old needs to go
jokes.punchline: Moira's dry, obvious response highlighting Johnny's absurdity
jokes.explanation: Solid straight-man response that lands well. Good economy and timing.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Where does a 92-year-old need to go?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: To the grave, John.

jokes.text: I think you've gotta raise that masculinity bar just... just a little.
jokes.setup: Moira praising Johnny's masculine approach to taking control
jokes.punchline: Johnny pointing out that begging for a truck to beg for money isn't very masculine
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor showing Johnny's recognition of his diminished circumstances.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: That was very masculine of you, taking control like that.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, I wouldn't say begging for a truck to beg for money is particularly masculine.

jokes.text: Lawnmower exchange with Roland not hearing
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to talk to Roland while he's mowing
jokes.punchline: Extended bit of miscommunication due to loud mower
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy bit, somewhat predictable but functional.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland, do you have a moment?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: What's that?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I said, do you have a moment to talk?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Can't hear you over the mower!

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: It's about the property line!

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: The property's fine!

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: No, the property LINE!

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I'll do another pass!

jokes.text: well, what can I do for you, you 'dumb son of a...'?
jokes.setup: Johnny calling Roland a 'dumb son of a...' when he thinks he can't hear
jokes.punchline: Roland heard it and playfully throws it back
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Roland's good nature and creates awkward moment for Johnny.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You dumb son of a...

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: What was that, Johnny?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Nothing, Roland.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: No, no, I heard you. You dumb son of a... what?

jokes.text: Roland's increasingly demanding truck conditions
jokes.setup: Roland graciously offering his truck
jokes.punchline: Endless list of specific requirements for returning it
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Roland's passive-aggressive nature. Each new demand gets more absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: You can borrow my truck, but I need it back by Friday.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: And it's gotta be filled up with gas. Premium, not regular.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: The tires need to be checked. All four of 'em.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: And I want the interior vacuumed. The seats wiped down.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: The cup holders cleaned out. And the air freshener replaced with the pine scent, not the vanilla.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: The windows washed inside and out. And the mirrors polished.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: And if there's even a spec of dust on the dashboard, we're gonna have words.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Or I could just... take my own car.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Your loss.

jokes.text: I guess your 'imaginary friend' uses wiper fluid, right?
jokes.setup: Johnny denying he uses wiper fluid
jokes.punchline: Roland's sarcastic response implying Johnny is lying
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Roland's petty passive-aggression in a clever way.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I don't use wiper fluid.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Oh sure, and I don't use it either. That's why my windshield's so clean.

jokes.text: No, it isn't! She didn't finish high school.
jokes.setup: MLM video claiming you can make $7,000 per week
jokes.punchline: Moira's dismissive comment about the host's education
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical joke showing Moira's elitist tendencies and mathematical skepticism.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, she clearly didn't make that money on her GED.

jokes.text: Look at the work she's had done, it's grotesque.
jokes.setup: Watching Justine's promotional video
jokes.punchline: Moira focusing on criticizing her friend's plastic surgery
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moira's cattiness, though somewhat predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, her face is certainly... different. I'm assuming that's a choice she made, and not a reaction to some sort of allergic situation.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: The cheekbones are very... pronounced. It's like watching someone who's been caught in a wind tunnel.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: And the lips! They're so plump, I'm worried she's going to tip over if she leans forward.

jokes.text: Okay, where are the tips and tricks? We've been watching this for five minutes, she's talking in circles!
jokes.setup: The MLM video promising tips and tricks
jokes.punchline: David's impatient realization they're getting nothing but circular sales speak
jokes.explanation: Relatable frustration with MLM marketing tactics, well-delivered impatience

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: So what are the tips and tricks?

dialogue.speaker: MLM Speaker (Video)
dialogue.line: Well, the key to success is to think like a successful person.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Right, but what does that mean?

dialogue.speaker: MLM Speaker (Video)
dialogue.line: It means you need to believe in yourself and your product.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, but specifically—

dialogue.speaker: MLM Speaker (Video)
dialogue.line: And once you believe, others will believe too.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Okay, but how do I actually make money?

dialogue.speaker: MLM Speaker (Video)
dialogue.line: By helping others succeed, you succeed.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm not getting anything from this.

jokes.text: Because it's a scam! / But if your father's willing to go out there and sell his soul...
jokes.setup: David asking why the video is talking in circles
jokes.punchline: Moira acknowledging it's a scam while deciding they should do it anyway
jokes.explanation: Excellent character hypocrisy - knowing it's wrong but doing it anyway for pragmatic reasons.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Wait, why is the video talking in circles?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: It's a scam, David.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: We should do it.

jokes.text: Okay, we're going to have a luncheon. / A luncheon?
jokes.setup: Moira announcing they'll sell the products
jokes.punchline: Her pretentious choice of 'luncheon' instead of lunch
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moira's pretentious language, David's reaction sells it.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: We're going to sell the products at a luncheon.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: A luncheon?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Yes, it's more sophisticated than a lunch.

jokes.text: First day of a cleanse, so she's a little whoo!
jokes.setup: Alexis being difficult about her breakfast order
jokes.punchline: Blaming her rude behavior on a cleanse
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Alexis making excuses for bad behavior, typical trendy wellness excuse.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, I'm being difficult. I'm on this new cleanse and I'm just really irritable.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: It's basically just hot water and lemon, but apparently it makes you a nightmare.

jokes.text: There's broken glass? / No, there is not broken glass in it
jokes.setup: Ted saying 'it's not like there's broken glass in it'
jokes.punchline: Twyla taking it literally and Ted having to clarify
jokes.explanation: Decent misunderstanding humor showing Twyla's literal-mindedness and Ted's awkwardness.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: It's not like there's broken glass in it.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Oh thank God. I thought you were going to say there was.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: No, no. I was just saying that to reassure you.

jokes.text: I do not like what this cleanse is doing to your brain. / You're being very rude!
jokes.setup: Ted trying to help with Alexis's difficult behavior
jokes.punchline: Alexis calling Ted rude for pointing out her rudeness
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic showing Alexis's lack of self-awareness and projection.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Alexis, I'm just trying to help. You've been incredibly rude to everyone today.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my god, that is so rude of you to say.

jokes.text: Ain't she a beaut? / Boy, is she ever, Bob.
jokes.setup: Bob proudly showing off his clearly terrible car
jokes.punchline: Johnny enthusiastically agreeing while obviously lying
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Johnny's desperation and the visual gag of the obviously terrible car.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Well, what do you think? Pretty great, right?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Oh, it's absolutely stunning. Really, truly one of the finest automobiles I've ever seen.

jokes.text: a little peccadillo of mine when I'm buying a car, I like to experience the car one on one
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to get rid of Bob for the test drive
jokes.punchline: Using the pretentious word 'peccadillo' and romantic language about cars
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Johnny's pretentious vocabulary and desperation to escape town.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Bob, I appreciate your enthusiasm, but I need you to understand something. A test drive is a very intimate experience. It's a moment between a man and a machine. A little peccadillo, if you will.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: A peccadillo is a minor fault or sin, Johnny.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Yes, well, the point is, I need to be alone with the car. It's romantic.

jokes.text: driving down that highway of life / feelin' fine and feelin' sunny / going to pick me up some money
jokes.setup: Johnny driving away in the car
jokes.punchline: His improvised song about going to collect unemployment
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Johnny's temporary optimism and ability to romanticize even unemployment.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: ♪ Going down to the unemployment office, gonna get myself some checks... ♪

jokes.text: stop being so generous and nice! / You pinned somebody against a locker?!
jokes.setup: Alexis complaining about Ted being too nice
jokes.punchline: Revealing she used to bully him and Stevie's shocked reaction
jokes.explanation: Excellent revelation that recontextualizes the relationship and shows Alexis's past behavior. Stevie's reaction sells it perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm just saying, Ted is too nice. It's exhausting. He's always so... helpful and considerate.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: What are you talking about? Ted's great.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I know, but like, in high school I was really mean to him. I called him names, I excluded him from things...

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: You bullied Ted?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, yes, but now he's so nice to me it just makes me feel guilty all the time.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Wow.

jokes.text: He always said hi when he passed me in the halls, and for some reason, it bugged me.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she bullied Ted
jokes.punchline: The completely irrational reason was him being polite
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work showing how Alexis's privilege made her interpret kindness as weakness. Perfectly irrational and very funny.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I bullied Ted because he was polite. Like, aggressively polite. He would hold doors open and say 'excuse me' and it was just... I couldn't stand it.

jokes.text: Car breakdown with Johnny's escalating profanity
jokes.setup: Johnny driving the obviously broken car
jokes.punchline: Car breaks down exactly as expected, Johnny's increasing anger
jokes.explanation: Predictable payoff but satisfying escalation of Johnny's frustration. Standard physical comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Come on, come on, come on...

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: No, no, no, no, NO!

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Are you KIDDING me right now?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: This is UNBELIEVABLE!

jokes.text: Okay, don't wanna tell me where we're going, interesting.
jokes.setup: Johnny being vague about his destination
jokes.punchline: Roland's passive-aggressive commentary on Johnny's privacy
jokes.explanation: Classic Roland needling - making Johnny's embarrassment worse through persistent questioning

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Oh, you're being all mysterious about where you're going? That's fine. I don't need to know. I'm just the guy who owns the motel where you're staying. But sure, keep your little secrets. I respect that.

jokes.text: The unemployment office! / Jeez pal, your business is your business. I don't know why you're telling me!
jokes.setup: Roland constantly pressing Johnny about where they're going
jokes.punchline: After finally getting an answer, Roland acts like he didn't want to know
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to Roland's nosiness. Great character consistency and timing. Roland's obliviousness is perfectly executed.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: So where are we going, Johnny? You've been so secretive about it.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: We're going to the hardware store, Roland.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: The hardware store? Why would you not just tell me that in the first place? I didn't even want to know!

jokes.text: The unemployment office! Okay? I'm applying for unemployment. Jeez pal, your business is your business. I don't know why you're telling me!
jokes.setup: Roland persistently asking where they're going
jokes.punchline: When Johnny finally tells him, Roland acts like he was prying inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roland manipulation - forces information out then acts offended by receiving it

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: So where are we going, Johnny?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland, I've told you three times already.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I know, I know, I'm just making conversation.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: We're going to the doctor.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa. That's personal, Johnny. I didn't need to know that.

jokes.text: David staging the fake surprise at the cosmetics
jokes.setup: Moira's staged MLM party presentation
jokes.punchline: David's obviously fake surprise at finding cosmetics everywhere
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing the family's desperate attempts at MLM theatrics. David's bad acting is the joke.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: And that's why Herb-scription cosmetics are not just a beauty product, they're a lifestyle choice!

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mom, why are there seventeen boxes of face serum under the sink?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David, I've explained this. I'm a regional sales associate now.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You bought seventeen boxes yesterday.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, you can never have too much inventory.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: And the forty jars of anti-wrinkle cream in your closet?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Those are samples for potential clients.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: There are literally products in the toilet tank.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh my God, David, why are you snooping in the toilet tank?

jokes.text: Can you not just say no for one time, Ted?!
jokes.setup: Ted being polite and agreeable about staying at the party
jokes.punchline: Alexis frustrated by his inability to be rude
jokes.explanation: Great callback to the earlier revelation about Ted's niceness. Shows Alexis's ongoing frustration with healthy behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Ted, you have to leave the party.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Oh, well, I'm actually having a really nice time.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Yes, but you need to go. Now.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Oh, but your friends have been so welcoming, and the appetizers are really—

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Ted! Can you just, for once in your life, be rude? Be mean? Say something horrible!

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I... I don't think I can do that.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: This is exactly what I'm talking about!

jokes.text: Now you get all decisive-y?
jokes.setup: Alexis complaining about Ted finally making a decision
jokes.punchline: Her made-up word 'decisive-y' to describe assertiveness
jokes.explanation: Good character voice with Alexis's valley girl speech patterns and invented words.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: He's being so decisive-y right now.

jokes.text: Alexis, you'd like this product. It's all about aging, and dry skin. Oh my God, ew, David!
jokes.setup: Moira trying to pitch anti-aging products to her daughter
jokes.punchline: Accidentally insulting Alexis by implying she needs anti-aging products
jokes.explanation: Moira's sales pitch backfires by insulting her target customer - her own daughter

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Alexis, darling, I've discovered the most revolutionary anti-aging serum. You simply must try it.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Mom, I'm in my twenties.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I know, sweetie. That's why you need to start now. Prevention is key.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Are you saying I look old?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: No, no. I'm saying you look like you're about to.

jokes.text: There's something wrong with your face.
jokes.setup: The failed MLM sales presentation
jokes.punchline: Ronnie's blunt observation delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ronnie delivery - cutting through the sales BS with brutal honesty

dialogue.speaker: Ronnie
dialogue.line: You people are delusional if you think anyone's gonna buy this crap. I've seen better sales pitches from a concussed squirrel.

jokes.text: Get some money? / I need a cheque, preferably today. Ideally in the next few minutes
jokes.setup: Johnny's entitled approach to unemployment office
jokes.punchline: His demanding timeline for government assistance
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Johnny's complete misunderstanding of how government assistance works.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I need to speak to someone about expediting my application. I have a very tight schedule.

dialogue.speaker: Clerk
dialogue.line: Sir, these things take time.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, I don't have time. I have a golf game on Thursday.

jokes.text: All my life. / And your reason for being out of work? / I sold my business, and I got screwed by my business manager.
jokes.setup: Bureaucratic interview questions
jokes.punchline: Johnny's answers that disqualify him without him realizing
jokes.explanation: Good bureaucratic comedy showing Johnny's privilege preventing him from understanding the system.

dialogue.speaker: Clerk
dialogue.line: Have you ever been convicted of a felony?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, not convicted.

dialogue.speaker: Clerk
dialogue.line: Do you have any experience in this field?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I have money. Isn't that experience?

dialogue.speaker: Clerk
dialogue.line: Can you start immediately?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm going to need at least three weeks to have my assistant set up my office. And I don't have an assistant yet.

jokes.text: I employed people, hundreds of people. / You see, and now I'm unemployed.
jokes.setup: Johnny not understanding why he doesn't qualify
jokes.punchline: His logic that being an employer should qualify him for employee benefits
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work showing Johnny's privileged worldview and complete misunderstanding of labor vs. capital.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm an employer. I have employees. Surely that qualifies me for employee benefits.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Dad, you can't get employee benefits for being an employer.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, that seems like a significant oversight in the system.

jokes.text: And, may I say, doing a fantastic job.
jokes.setup: Johnny asking for the supervisor and being told this person IS the supervisor
jokes.punchline: His sarcastic compliment to the person who just denied his claim
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic moment showing Johnny's frustration while maintaining his businessman persona.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, you're doing a wonderful job.

jokes.text: Get out! / I know someone here wants in on this exclusive opportunity!
jokes.setup: The MLM party completely failing
jokes.punchline: Moira's delusional persistence despite obvious failure
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Moira's inability to read the room and desperate denial.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I think it went rather well, actually. Did you see how engaged everyone was? Particularly when they were leaving.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: And the energy in the room was electric. Granted, most of it was static electricity from people frantically gathering their belongings, but energy nonetheless.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm already planning the next event. I'm thinking we go bigger. More aggressive. Perhaps we hold them hostage until they commit to a starter kit.

jokes.text: Sorry, you're... you're an Allez Vous consultant? / I'm actually an executive area manager. / I'm a district manager. / I'm an executive district manager. / I gave up.
jokes.setup: Learning the townspeople are already in the MLM
jokes.punchline: Escalating fake titles followed by defeat
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing the absurdity of MLM titles, perfectly timed with the final deflation. Great ensemble work.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Wait, you're all already in an MLM?

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Oh yes, I'm a Regional Vice President.

dialogue.speaker: Ronnie
dialogue.line: I'm a District Manager.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I'm a National Executive Director of Operations.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: I'm actually a Continental Ambassador.

dialogue.speaker: Ronnie
dialogue.line: Global Empress.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm the... Supreme Overlord of All Pyramids.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: That's not real.

dialogue.speaker: Ronnie
dialogue.line: Yeah, Moira, that's not a thing.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh.

jokes.text: In two weeks we had sold everything to the other half of town, and vice versa. / That's... that's funny. / It became a bit incestuous, yeah, very small sales pool.
jokes.setup: Explaining how the MLM worked in a small town
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of everyone selling to each other in circles
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational humor about small-town dynamics and MLM absurdity. The word 'incestuous' is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: In a town this size, the multi-level marketing structure becomes rather... recursive.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: What do you mean?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I was selling to Roland, who was selling to David, who was selling to you, who was selling back to me.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: So we were all just... buying from each other?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: In circles. We created a pyramid scheme where the pyramid was actually a circle, and we were all at the bottom of it.

jokes.text: Marnie and I were, um, how shall I say it? 'Close friends' in high school. / Isn't that right, Marnie? / Marnie likes the bad boys.
jokes.setup: Roland encountering his high school hookup
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate oversharing and her obvious discomfort
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Roland's lack of social awareness and tendency to overshare.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Marnie! Oh my God, it's been what, thirty years? You look exactly the same. Well, not exactly. Your face has changed. And your body. But in a good way, I think. Anyway, I've thought about you a lot over the years. Mostly in the shower. Sometimes in the car. One time during a city council meeting, but I was very discreet about it.

dialogue.speaker: Marnie
dialogue.line: Oh. Um. That's... nice?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: You know, I still have the motel receipt from that night. I keep it in my wallet. Next to my driver's license and pictures of my kids, but closer to the front.

dialogue.speaker: Marnie
dialogue.line: I should probably get going.

jokes.text: Oh well, didn't he tell you about the job he was doing for me? / You didn't tell her? / Well, that's important information, Mr. Rose.
jokes.setup: Johnny not qualifying for unemployment
jokes.punchline: Roland suddenly 'remembering' Johnny worked for him
jokes.explanation: Excellent plot development showing Roland helping Johnny while getting to humiliate him. Great character dynamics.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I don't qualify for unemployment. I need to find work immediately.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Wait a minute... didn't you work for me at the store?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: No, Roland, I never worked for you.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Oh right, right, right... I'm remembering it now. You worked for me for... two years.

jokes.text: he was my assistant, he was like my little guy Friday. He would fetch things for me, I'd tell him where to go and he'd go running off
jokes.setup: Roland helping Johnny qualify for unemployment
jokes.punchline: Making up humiliating job duties for Johnny
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Roland's revenge for all of Johnny's condescension. 'Little guy Friday' is perfectly humiliating.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: So you need to list your job duties for the unemployment office.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Yes, that's right.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, let's see... You were in charge of... moisturizing the storefront windows with your own saliva.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: What? No, I wasn't!

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: And twice a week you had to polish the merchandise with your hair.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland, I'm not putting that down!

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Oh, and your primary responsibility was standing in the window in your underwear to attract customers.

jokes.text: because of that whole 'cut the gravy' thing, so.
jokes.setup: Roland explaining why he had to fire Johnny
jokes.punchline: Referencing an off-screen incident about cutting gravy
jokes.explanation: Great throwaway line that implies an entire backstory. Perfect economy and very quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I had to let Johnny go. He was cutting the gravy with water.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I said, 'Johnny, you can't cut the gravy with water.' He said, 'Why not?' I said, 'Because then it's not gravy, it's just hot salt water with the dreams of gravy in it.'

jokes.text: Hey... I come in peace. What's this? I don't know, it's a pre shave follicle mask or something, I took it from the pile of product that nobody bought.
jokes.setup: Alexis apologizing to Ted with a peace offering
jokes.punchline: The gift is random unsold MLM product she grabbed
jokes.explanation: Shows Alexis's half-hearted attempt at thoughtfulness using whatever's available

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Ted, I'm so sorry. I got you a gift as an apology.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Oh, Alexis, that's very thoughtful of you.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I know you're going to love it. It's this amazing skincare line that I've been meaning to get rid of—I mean, share with you.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Is this... an MLM product?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, it was sitting in my closet and nobody wanted it, so...

jokes.text: You have to understand that you're the first nice guy that I've dated. / They don't like me? / No, they do! They do, they've just always seen nice as a sign of weakness
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining her family's reaction to Ted
jokes.punchline: Admitting her family sees kindness as weakness
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing Alexis's growing self-awareness about her family's dysfunction.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: My family sees kindness as a weakness. Like, my dad literally told me that showing emotion is for people who can't afford therapy.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: That's... that's not healthy.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I know! But try telling that to a man who considers eye contact a personal attack.

jokes.text: Say something super mean. / What, I can't just turn it on in an instant, like you can.
jokes.setup: Alexis asking Ted to be mean as role play
jokes.punchline: Ted's observation that meanness comes naturally to Alexis
jokes.explanation: Excellent observation about Alexis's character delivered with perfect timing. Great insight into their dynamic.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Can you just be mean to me? Like, as a roleplay thing?

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I don't think I need to roleplay that with you.

jokes.text: Well, and you, what about you? Are you just going to sit around all day, while I work and buy you dinners? You've gotta go out there and get a job... / Too far. / It's too far, okay.
jokes.setup: Ted trying to be mean as requested
jokes.punchline: Going too far by hitting on actual relationship issues
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation where the role play becomes too real. Perfect character work showing Ted's suppressed frustrations.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Just try to be mean to me.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. You're shallow, you're self-absorbed, and you've never had a real relationship that lasted more than a few months because you get bored the second someone actually cares about you.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Wow, okay, that's... that's actually really mean.

jokes.text: Well um, one day, and that day may never come, I may call upon you to perform a function. / Marlon Brando! / Yes, but from what movie? / The Godfather. / No, it's the one where he's the big mafia guy. / The Godfather!
jokes.setup: Roland doing a Godfather impression
jokes.punchline: Johnny recognizing it but Roland not knowing it's the same movie
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Roland's cultural confusion and Johnny's exasperation.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I'm gonna make you an offer you can't refuse.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Is that The Godfather?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: The what?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: The Godfather. The movie.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: No, no. This is just how I talk now.

jokes.text: Well um, one day, and that day may never come, I may call upon you to perform a function. Marlon Brando! Yes, but from what movie? The Godfather. No, it's the one where he's the big mafia guy. The Godfather!
jokes.setup: Roland quoting The Godfather
jokes.punchline: Johnny recognizing it but Roland not knowing which movie despite describing The Godfather
jokes.explanation: Roland's cultural ignorance showing through his attempt to be sophisticated

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I'm gonna make him an offer he can't refuse.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: That's The Godfather.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: No, no, no. It's not a movie. It's something I came up with.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland, that's literally The Godfather.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, I don't know what that is.

jokes.text: Why don't you just get me a little wiper fluid, and we'll call it even, okay? And also maybe a Kleenex caddy? I've always wanted one of those. That's funny! It is, but I'm not joking.
jokes.setup: Roland asking for small repayment favors
jokes.punchline: Adding Kleenex caddy to the list and clarifying he's serious
jokes.explanation: Callbacks to Roland's earlier petty requests, shows his consistency

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: So I need you to do me a favor. I need you to pick up some Kleenex for me.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Of course.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: And I need you to grab me a caddy for it.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Sure.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I'm serious.

jokes.text: Oh Johnny, I told you not to take 'er on the highway. / Yeah, I'm sorry, Bob
jokes.setup: Johnny's car breaking down again
jokes.punchline: Bob's 'I told you so' moment about the highway
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the earlier highway warning, though somewhat predictable payoff.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: I told you that car wasn't going to make it on the highway.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, it made it further than I expected.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: It broke down two miles from here.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Exactly. Two miles further than I expected.

jokes.text: I'll knock twenty bucks off. / You couldn't get twenty bucks for scrap! / All right, thirty bucks! / How about zero bucks?! / I wouldn't give you a dime for this! / Johnny, you're killing me here! / Fifty bucks!
jokes.setup: Negotiating over the clearly worthless car
jokes.punchline: Bob raising his offer despite Johnny's insults
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Bob's delusional salesmanship and backwards negotiation tactics.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: This car is a complete disaster. It's falling apart, Bob.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: I'm aware of the condition, but I'm willing to offer you fifteen thousand dollars.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Fifteen thousand? Bob, you couldn't give this away. It's worthless.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Well, I think you're being unfair. I'm going to raise my offer to seventeen thousand.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You're insane. The transmission doesn't even work!

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Eighteen thousand. Final offer.

jokes.text: I'll throw in a couple of floor mats, but that's my final offer! / Walking!
jokes.setup: Bob's final desperate sales pitch
jokes.punchline: Offering floor mats with a broken car as Johnny walks away
jokes.explanation: Good escalation capper showing the absurdity of adding accessories to a worthless car.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait! Johnny, come back! I'll throw in these premium floor mats absolutely free!

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Bob, the car doesn't have an engine.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Yes, but look at how clean the floors will be.

tmdb_overview: Moira and David try to earn money by selling cosmetics to the locals, while Johnny heads to Elmdale in hopes of collecting unemployment insurance.
insight: Character-driven cringe sustains a slower burn: 54 jokes in 26 minutes, mostly on David's social anxiety.
